config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menindas kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil terhadap setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang mampu menyeimbangkan keduanya, bukan mengutamakan satu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can become an excuse to trample free speech, due process, and minority rights. At the same time, individual rights are not a blank check to ignore public safety, public health, or basic rules that let people live together. The right balance is that society can limit individual freedom only when there is a clear, necessary, and narrowly targeted reason, not just because the majority wants it. If we get that balance wrong, the people with the least power are usually the ones who pay for it.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman Indonesia, saya justru melihat bahwa alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara kelompok yang lemah paling mudah dirugikan. Kepentingan bersama memang perlu, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, prosedur yang adil, dan pengawasan yang ketat agar tidak berubah jadi alat penindasan. Jadi, bagi saya, yang benar bukan mengalahkan individu demi masyarakat, melainkan menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse of power. The point about public safety matters, but that does not mean the majority gets to override basic rights whenever it is convenient or popular. I would only accept limits on individual freedom when they are narrowly tailored, necessary, and accountable, because otherwise “society” becomes a vague excuse for trampling the people who are already least protected.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sewenang-wenang. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasan harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan diawasi ketat, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas atau penguasa ingin cepat selesai. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang paling menderita biasanya kelompok kecil dan lemah yang suaranya paling tidak terdengar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang adil justru yang melindungi hak dasar setiap orang sambil tetap mencari kepentingan bersama secara bertanggung jawab.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, that kind of language has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Public safety and public health do matter, but limits on individual rights should be narrow, necessary, and accountable, not based on whatever the majority finds convenient. When society is allowed to outrank the individual too easily, the people with the least power are usually the first to lose their voice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
